up.method: measured nitrate uptake assay (nitrogen-limitation management); numeric value reported only in figure/image, not extractable from text

growth_records.cite: Nadia Jezequel et al. Long-Term Diversity and Genome Adaptation of Acinetobacter baylyi in a Minimal-Medium Chemostat. Genome Biology and Evolution (2013). DOI:10.1093/gbe/evs120. [PMC3595037]
growth_records.snippet: The bacteria were grown with limited amounts of nitrate as the sole source of nitrogen. A high dilution rate (between 0.6 h-1 and 0.9 h-1)... it was kept constant between 0.6 h-1 and 0.9 h-1. ... a dilution rate of approximately 80% of maxi
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state: growth_rate = dilution_rate (mu = D). Operating dilution rate reported as a range 0.6-0.9 h-1; midpoint 0.75 h-1 used for the single fields, full range documented here (during first 6 days D was kept <0.5 h-1 to avoid washout). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.75 = 0.924 h. mu ~= 80% of mu_max per authors. Maximum growth rate (mu_max), nitrate uptake, and starvation resistance were experimentally measured but their numeric values are embedded as inline-formula images (mu symbols) not present as machine-readable text. Citrate (3 mM potassium citrate + 9 mM citric acid) is the carbon source; nitrate (1 mM) is the growth-limiting nitrogen source. Batch mu_max measured in a custom LED/photodetector OD vessel.
